top_signals.title: ECB’s Nagel Says He'll Stay Open-Minded for Next Two Meetings
top_signals.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Joachim Nagel says he’s keeping an open mind on what action is needed at the next two interest-rate meetings. "The geopolitical situation is still uncertain," Nagel tells Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the ECB Forum on Central Banking in Sintra, Portugal. "We have to wait." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: EU's 'Historic' Steel Measures Will Help Preserve Jobs, Says Eurofer Director-General
top_signals.summary: The EU's stricter steel import quotas will help preserve 200,000 local jobs, Director-General of the European steel association Eurofer Axel Eggert told BTV on Wednesday. The industry has been struggling with high energy costs and global competition. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Germany’s Pension Fund Would Be Safe With Bundesbank, Nagel Says
top_signals.summary: Germany’s central bank is well positioned to oversee the country’s planned state-backed pension fund if it’s asked to do so, according to President Joachim Nagel.
